# Estimating the Total Combined Age of Iron Maiden Birthday Attendees

## Building the Estimate Step by Step

### 1. Total Concerts (1982–2024)
Iron Maiden is one of the most prolific touring acts in rock history. Cataloguing their major tours (Beast on the Road, World Slavery, Somewhere on Tour, Seventh Son, the 90s tours, the post-reunion era, Legacy of the Beast, Future Past, etc.), I estimate roughly **~2,200 concerts** from March 1982 to mid-2025.

### 2. Average Attendance per Show
Iron Maiden consistently fills arenas (10,000–20,000) and headlines major festivals. The Blaze Bayley era saw smaller crowds; the reunion era saw massive ones. A weighted average across all eras gives roughly **~15,000 per show**.

### 3. Total Person-Attendances
$$2{,}200 \times 15{,}000 = 33{,}000{,}000$$

### 4. Probability of Attending on Your Birthday
$$\frac{1}{365.25} \approx 0.00274$$

### 5. Number of Birthday Attendees
$$33{,}000{,}000 \times 0.00274 \approx 90{,}300 \text{ people}$$

### 6. Average Age of Attendees (Weighted Across Eras)
- **1982–1989**: Younger fanbase, avg age ~22
- **1990–1999**: Maturing fanbase, avg age ~28
- **2000–2024**: Aging core + younger recruits, avg age ~38

Weighted by the person-attendances in each era, the overall average comes to roughly **~33 years old**.

### 7. Final Calculation
$$90{,}300 \times 33 \approx 2{,}980{,}000$$

---

## My Estimate: **3,000,000 years**
